app.name: Seelen UI
app.one_liner: A fully customizable desktop environment replacement for Windows that reimagines core shell components like the taskbar, dock, and window manager through deep theming and extensible widgets.

screens.name: Configuration Panel
screens.purpose: Allows users to adjust shell behavior, enable/disable widgets, manage themes, and configure input/display settings.

screens.name: Desktop Shell
screens.purpose: Provides a customizable replacement for the Windows desktop, including taskbar, dock, app launcher, and virtual desktops.

rust_commands.name: handle_display_change
rust_commands.purpose: Responds to monitor connection/disconnection events to maintain UI stability across dynamic display configurations.

rust_commands.name: manage_alt_tab_focus
rust_commands.purpose: Ensures proper focus handling during Alt-Tab switching, especially when interacting with fullscreen applications or configuration panels.

rust_commands.name: process_touchpad_gesture
rust_commands.purpose: Interprets touchpad tap-to-click and gesture inputs correctly within the Seelen UI shell context.

rust_commands.name: toggle_toolbar_visibility
rust_commands.purpose: Controls the appearance and disappearance of the toolbar and dock based on user settings and system state (e.g., game mode).

mvp_plan: week 1: Ensure consistent toolbar/dock visibility logic across sessions
week 1: Fix Alt-Tab focus retention during shell interactions
week 1: Implement stable display hot-plug detection and UI reconfiguration
week 1: Validate touchpad gesture recognition in all input contexts
acceptance_tests: Alt-Tab selector does not get stuck after switching focus from the Seelen UI configuration panel [E-IS-004].
Toolbar and dock do not intermittently disappear during normal desktop usage [E-IS-006].
Toolbar and dock remain stable when connecting or disconnecting external displays [E-IS-002].
Touchpad tap-to-click gestures are consistently recognized without misinterpretation [E-IS-003].
